title: Beginner Agentic Workflow Design and Capstone Showcase

learning_objectives: Combine all previous weeks into a practical capstone.

blocks.title: Week 10 Focus
blocks.content: Students define the problem, workflow, tools, prompts, risks, and review points for a final showcase.

items.label: Problem Framing
items.description: State the user, pain point, and goal clearly.

items.label: Workflow Quality
items.description: Show a logical, connected sequence of steps.

items.label: Risk Control
items.description: Explain what can go wrong and how review helps.

items.label: Workflow
items.description: The sequence of inputs, AI steps, outputs, and review.

items.label: Risks and Controls
items.description: Limitations, privacy concerns, and human oversight.

blocks.title: Final Standard
blocks.content: A strong capstone is practical, understandable, and honest about where human review is needed.

activity.title: Capstone Showcase
activity.instructions: Prepare a beginner-friendly AI workflow with user, problem, tools, prompts, risks, and human review.
activity.success_criteria: Submit a coherent workflow with clear value and safety considerations.

questions.prompt: What should a strong capstone begin with?
questions.explanation: A strong capstone starts with a clear problem, user, and purpose.

questions.prompt: Why include risks and controls in a capstone?
options: To show responsible design and human oversight
questions.explanation: Risks and controls show that the solution is realistic and responsible.

questions.prompt: Which item belongs in a capstone presentation?
questions.explanation: A workflow diagram helps explain the logic and flow of the proposed solution.

reflection.prompt: Summarize your capstone idea in 4 to 6 lines. Include the user, the problem, the AI role, and the human review point.
reflection.placeholder: My capstone helps students turn notes into study checklists. AI summarizes the notes and extracts key terms, then the student reviews the checklist before using it.

teacher_notes: Evaluate whether the project solves a real problem before focusing on polish.
Push students to name risks precisely, not just say 'AI can be wrong'.
